title: ApprovalDecision
description: B-department signed decision on a ChangePlan (Action Contract). Authority path per ADR-0003: B signed approval -> Policy Gate pre-verification (policy.decision.recorded.v1) -> plan-contract-service signals Temporal directly; plan.contract.approved.v1 is notification-only.
$comment: compat_class: closed, signed: true. Authority: approved plan §2 domain row 2 (docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2, §5); docs/architecture/contract-catalog.md P0 table 'ApprovalDecision' row (Owner plan-contract, Idempotency key 'contract_hash+approver actor_id', Sensitivity PII+internal); ADR-0003 (docs/decisions/ADR-0003-approval-transition-authority-path.md) authority path. H-7 dual-approval semantics: docs/architecture/security-model.md '승인 원자성 해체 (H-7)' — 'per-patch-unit secret lease ... 고위험 unit 2인 승인'. Each approver submits ONE ApprovalDecision instance; idempotency key = contract_hash + approver_actor_id (contract-catalog.md), so a dual-approval requirement is represented as two separate instances of this contract sharing the same contract_hash, NOT as a multi-approver array inside a single instance. The relational check that both required approver instances exist (a k-anonymity-precedent-style cross-instance aggregate check) is a policy-gate concern, deferred to W2A — not expressible in a single-instance JSON Schema.

contract_hash.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list; contract-catalog.md idempotency key component. Points at the ChangePlan instance this decision resolves — ChangePlan itself deliberately omits its own hash (self-reference avoidance, see change-plan.schema.json $comment); this is the primary carrier.

tenant_id.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list. Single-authority identifier convention (ADR-0011/ADR-0014).

run_id.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list.

approver_actor_id.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list; contract-catalog.md idempotency key component 'approver actor_id'. Note: ruling R9 (Security MUST-FIX) removes approver_actor_id from the plan.contract.approved.v1 EVENT payload (event/plan-contract-approved is a separate w1-08 file, contract_hash-reference only) — that removal does NOT apply to this domain contract itself, which is the PII-sensitive system-of-record instance (contract-catalog.md Sensitivity 'PII+internal'); the event is a thin notification projection of it.

decision.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list.

patch_unit_decisions.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list — per-patch-unit granularity, consistent with H-7 per-patch-unit secret lease separation (security-model.md).

signature.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list. Opaque detached value — W1 explicitly declares signature VERIFICATION is not guaranteed/implemented at this layer ('검증 미보장'); full semantics (key management, verification pipeline) are W2A + ADR-0003 'Open decisions: 서명 체계 (키 관리, 서명 알고리즘) — 별도 설계 문서'. Identity-embedding signature formats (e.g. PGP armored blocks carrying a User ID) are structurally forbidden by using an opaque string rather than a structured/parseable envelope — Security SHOULD-7 (PII minimization: identity must not be embedded inside the signature value itself, only referenced via approver_actor_id).

signature_algorithm.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list. Algorithm identifier is open-string in W1 (no fixed enum) pending the ADR-0003 signature-scheme design doc.

decided_at.$comment: docs/... virtual-cooking-map.md §2 domain row 2 field list.
